element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player places initial residential zones to establish population base
Introduced once residential foundation exists; requires adjacent roads and power
Produces pollution; player must balance economic benefit against environmental cost
Required for zoned areas to function; coal plants vs. nuclear trade-off introduces strategic tension
Fundamental prerequisite; player learns road connectivity enables zoning
Player must balance revenue against services; poor decisions create spiral toward bankruptcy
Random fires, floods, earthquakes test city resilience; loss condition emerges
Coverage and funding directly impact zone value and population happiness

typical_tension_triggers: Crime or pollution reaching critical levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle tension with player-driven pacing; slow-burn economic pressure punctuated by sudden discrete disaster events; tension primarily emergent from systemic imbalance rather than time pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Higher density zoning and advanced features (mass transit, airports) unlocked by city growth milestones; player discovers these incrementally

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very forgiving; abundant starting funds allow experimentation; no immediate consequences for poor planning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Economic systems become constraining; player must manage competing resource demands; first disaster events appear
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex balancing act; large cities generate multiple simultaneous pressure points; one poor decision cascades into financial collapse
notable_spikes: First major disaster event introduces unpredictable loss condition
Population growth reaching zone service capacity limits
Budget deficit tipping into unrecoverable deficit spiral

notable_level_patterns.description: SimCity uses no scripted levels. Player incremental mastery follows emergent system complexity: basic zoning → balanced economy → disaster management → optimization. Difficulty determined by player's self-imposed constraints and random event frequency.
Early game has slack budget allowing errors. Mid-game introduces tighter margins. Late-game cities demand constant optimization; small inefficiencies compound into crisis.
